Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Defective endogenous OsDFR identifies target backgrounds requiring structural gene complementation for anthocyanin biofortification in rice endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins using NADPH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defective OsDFR in ZH11/HG1 prevents completion of anthocyanin biosynthesis; necessitated introduction of SsDFR.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequence analysis in white rice varieties; engineering complementation logic supported by transgenic phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Rice endosperm in developing seeds; white-grain rice backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Assigned by conserved anthocyanin pathway annotation and prior literature; not biochemically assayed here.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Known anthocyanin pathway step between dihydroflavonols and leucoanthocyanidins; loss of endogenous function motivated complementation with SsDFR.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate-gene sequence analysis in rice varieties.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: ZH11 and HG1 sequence comparison context.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Defective allele in ZH11/HG1; specific lesion not given in provided text.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Anthocyanins quantified by HPLC in engineered lines.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Endogenous OsDFR was defective in both transformation backgrounds, so SsDFR was introduced from Coleus.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data for endosperm expression; accessions NA.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Defective endogenous allele associated with no anthocyanin accumulation in white rice endosperm.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful structural transgene to boost anthocyanin flux in rice endosperm beyond endogenous OsCHS activation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in engineered rice endosperm; lines lacking SsCHS showed lighter purple grains and lower anthocyanin content.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression, genotype–phenotype comparison of lines lacking the transgene, HPLC anthocyanin analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Developing and mature rice endosperm under endosperm-specific transgene expression.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Gene cloned from Coleus based on conserved pathway sequence; standard enzyme assignment.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Canonical first committed flavonoid step; absence lowered end-product anthocyanin despite activation of endogenous OsCHS.
Pathway_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Presence of functional endosperm-expressed SsCHS transgene
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RT-PCR amplification with degenerate primers and RACE from Coleus cDNA.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Transgenic PER lines including lighter purple lines Z#1, Z#2, H#11 lacking this gene.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Absent in lighter-purple lines Z#1, Z#2, H#11
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC for anthocyanins; TEAC antioxidant assay
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Even though endogenous OsCHS was activated, extra transgene enhanced anthocyanin levels.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-PCR expression analysis in PER; accessions NA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Lighter grain color and lower antioxidant activity when absent in some lines.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General flavonoid entry step leading to anthocyanin branch
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of multigene stack associated with ~25% lower grain weight in PER lines overall.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Absence associated with visibly lighter purple grains and reduced anthocyanin/antioxidant levels.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful structural transgene to strengthen anthocyanin pathway flux in cereal endosperm for biofortification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation in engineered endosperm; absence contributed to lighter purple grains and lower anthocyanin levels.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression plus genotype–phenotype comparison of lines missing SsCHI; HPLC anthocyanin measurement.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Developing seed endosperm under endosperm-specific promoters.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Standard pathway enzyme assignment from cloned Coleus cDNA.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Canonical early flavonoid pathway step; endogenous OsCHI was activated but transgene absence still lowered anthocyanin output.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RT-PCR amplification with degenerate primers and RACE from Coleus.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: PER lines Z#1, Z#2, H#11 lacking this and two other structural genes.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Absent in lighter-purple lines Z#1, Z#2, H#11
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Reduced pigmentation/intensity when absent in partial-transgene lines.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General flavonoid pathway to anthocyanin branch
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of multigene engineering package associated with lower grain weight overall.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Absence associated with lighter purple grains and lower anthocyanin/antioxidant activity.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Critical structural transgene to complete anthocyanin pathway where endogenous OsF3H is not activated in endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides essential hydroxylation step enabling anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm because endogenous OsF3H was not activated.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression with RT-PCR evidence that endogenous OsF3H was not activated; anthocyanin accumulation in full engineered lines.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Standard pathway annotation from cloned Coleus cDNA; known anthocyanin enzyme class.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Authors note endogenous OsF3H was not activated in ZH11 or PER, so transgenic SsF3H helped complete pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RT-PCR amplification with degenerate primers and RACE from Coleus.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Engineered PER lines in ZH11/HG1 backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Transferred because endogenous OsF3H remained silent even in PER.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No standalone value reported; required in engineered pathway completion.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Structural transgene to channel flux toward cyanidin-derived anthocyanins in rice endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid B-ring at the 3' position, converting substrates such as dihydrokaempferol/naringenin-derived intermediates toward dihydroquercetin/cyanidin branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances cyanidin-derived anthocyanin production; absence in some lines reduced pigmentation intensity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression, genotype–phenotype comparison in lines missing transgene, HPLC anthocyanin analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Developing rice endosperm with endosperm-specific expression.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Standard enzyme assignment from cloned Coleus cDNA.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Canonical B-ring hydroxylation step supplying cyanidin branch; endogenous OsF3'H was activated, but extra transgene increased output.
Pathway_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Presence of functional SsF3' H transgene
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RT-PCR amplification with degenerate primers and RACE from Coleus.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: PER lines including Z#1, Z#2, H#11 lacking this transgene.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Absent in lighter-purple lines Z#1, Z#2, H#11
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Text spells gene as F3'OH/F30 H due formatting; corresponds to flavonoid 3'-hydroxylase.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Reduced purple intensity and antioxidant activity when absent with SsCHS/SsCHI.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Absence associated with lighter purple grains and lower anthocyanin content.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Essential complementation transgene for defective endogenous OsDFR, enabling anthocyanin biofortification in white rice backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for engineered anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm; lines lacking SsDFR did not produce purple seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic complementation and genotype–phenotype evidence from H#12 lacking SsDFR; HPLC anthocyanin profiling in PER lines.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Developing and mature rice endosperm under endosperm-specific expression.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Canonical DFR pathway assignment from cloned Coleus cDNA.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Complements defective endogenous OsDFR; loss abolishes purple seed phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RT-PCR amplification with degenerate primers and RACE from Coleus.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: PER line H#12 lacking SsDFR; ZH11/HG1 transgenic backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Critical because endogenous OsDFR is defective in transformation backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-PCR expression analysis in PER; accessions NA.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Absence caused complete loss of purple seed phenotype in H#12.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Critical structural transgene to complete anthocyanin pathway where endogenous OsANS is not activated in rice endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins such as cyanidin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables anthocyanidin formation in engineered rice endosperm because endogenous OsANS remained unactivated.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression plus RT-PCR showing endogenous OsANS not activated; anthocyanin accumulation in engineered lines.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Canonical pathway annotation from cloned Coleus cDNA.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Endogenous OsANS was not activated; transgenic SsANS therefore completes downstream anthocyanidin formation step.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RT-PCR amplification with degenerate primers and RACE from Coleus.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Required because endogenous OsANS remained silent in ZH11 and PER.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No standalone effect quantified; part of required complete gene set.

_comments.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: 这个字段是否需要？我们的目的是输出结果，不是呈现实验过程。是否可以将该字段改为基因表达特征？其它基因同理。
_comments.Metabolic_Step_Position: 需要具体说明是哪个代谢通路，在单篇文献研究中可以说是上游、下游，但是如果放到整个营养合成或次级代谢，这种描述就不够准确了。其它基因同理。

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Endogenous structural gene that can be activated by regulators, but supplemental transgene improved output; useful but insufficient alone for strong endosperm biofortification.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated/upregulated in PER endosperm, contributing to anthocyanin synthesis, but endogenous activation alone was insufficient for maximal pigment accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semi-quantitative RT-PCR and genotype–phenotype inference from lines lacking supplemental SsCHS.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known rice flavonoid pathway enzyme assignment.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Activated by rebuilt MBW complex in PER; positioned at flavonoid entry step.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Endogenous gene expression analysis in engineered and control endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Presence of ZmLc/ZmPl transgenes in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Activated endogenous gene, yet authors conclude transgenic SsCHS still enhanced anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data and RT-PCR; accessions NA.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Activated/upregulated in PER relative to ZH11; exact fold change NA.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Endogenous pathway enzyme activated by regulators, but transgenic supplementation improved final anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated in PER endosperm and contributes to anthocyanin synthesis, but not sufficient alone for strong pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semi-quantitative RT-PCR; comparison with lines lacking supplemental SsCHI.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Activated downstream of MBW regulation in PER; early flavonoid step.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Supplemented by SsCHI to raise anthocyanin levels.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Activated/upregulated in PER; exact fold not reported.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Endogenous branch enzyme activated in PER; contributes to cyanidin-pathway flux but transgene supplementation improved output.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid B-ring at the 3' position to produce cyanidin-branch intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated/upregulated in PER and contributes to cyanidin-derived anthocyanin production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semi-quantitative RT-PCR and engineering logic with supplemental SsF3' H.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Activated by engineered MBW and positioned at cyanidin branch choice step.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Formatting in paper appears as OsF30 H.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Activated/upregulated in PER; exact value NA.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Endogenous structural gene not useful alone in white rice endosperm because not activated even after regulatory engineering; justified use of heterologous SsF3H.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not activated in ZH11 or PER endosperm, so did not contribute to engineered anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Known midstream anthocyanin step; lack of expression in PER indicated pathway gap.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Expression analysis of endogenous pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Indirect via RT-PCR and engineered metabolite output
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: One of four functional endogenous genes not activated by regulatory genes.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No activation detected in both ZH11 and PER-Z#3.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Endogenous structural gene remained inactive in endosperm, highlighting need for heterologous ANS transgene in anthocyanin biofortification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not activated in wild-type or PER endosperm; therefore did not support engineered anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Known downstream anthocyanin step; persistent silence identified a gap requiring SsANS.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Indirect via RT-PCR and HPLC of engineered lines
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: One of four functional endogenous genes not activated.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No activation detected in ZH11 or PER.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Endogenous branch enzyme not activated in endosperm; indicates limited diversification branch and supports focused cyanidin production engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavonoid B-ring at both 3' and 5' positions to generate delphinidin-branch precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not activated in ZH11 or PER endosperm, consistent with limited anthocyanin diversity and predominance of cyanidin/peonidin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semi-quantitative RT-PCR and metabolite profile showing only C3G/P3G detected.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Represents alternative hydroxylation branch to delphinidin-type products; absence of activation aligns with observed product spectrum.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Not activated by regulatory genes in both ZH11 and PER.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No activation detected in ZH11 or PER.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Indicates minor branch competition with anthocyanin pathway; not activated in engineered endosperm, helping favor anthocyanin over catechin/proanthocyanidin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not activated in ZH11 or PER endosperm; minor proanthocyanidin branch remained limited.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semi-quantitative RT-PCR with supporting metabolite observation of only low proanthocyanidins in PER.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Alternative fate of leucoanthocyanidins; lack of activation supports anthocyanin accumulation over proanthocyanidin branch.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: RT-PCR; HPLC detected low levels of proanthocyanidins in PER
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Authors note minor proanthocyanidin branch not shown in model figure.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No activation detected; only low proanthocyanidins observed in PER endosperm.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful diagnostic target for selecting rice backgrounds that cannot autonomously support anthocyanin accumulation in endosperm; defect indicates need for transgenic complementation/regulatory reconstruction for anthocyanin biofortification.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Anthocyanin regulatory role relevant to pigmented tissues; in white-grained rice and endosperm the gene is defective and/or inactive for anthocyanin production.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defective OsB1 in white rice backgrounds is associated with failure to support anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequence analysis across rice varieties; inference from prior functional literature cited in paper.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the native bHLH regulatory module for anthocyanin production, but not used as an effective endogenous regulator in this engineering strategy because it is defective in target varieties.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Rice endosperm and pigmented rice tissues; discussed in context of engineering developing seed endosperm.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct regulator as component of MBW transcriptional complex in anthocyanin pathway, based on prior literature context.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Functional OsB1 allele (not present in ZH11/HG1)
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate-gene sequence analysis of anthocyanin pathway genes in different rice varieties.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Rice varieties including Zhonghua 11 (ZH11) and Huaguang 1 (HG1); comparative sequence analysis.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Interacts with OsC1 and OsWD40 as part of MBW complex; homologous to maize Booster1-type bHLH regulators.
Regulation_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Defective allele reported, specific lesion not given in provided text.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Indirect in this paper; anthocyanins assayed by HPLC in engineered lines.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as native rice regulator homologous to maize Booster1; not directly transformed in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data analyzed for endosperm expression; accession numbers NA.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as part of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes when in functional MBW complex

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Defect in white rice backgrounds indicates this endogenous regulator is unsuitable alone for anthocyanin biofortification; informs choice of transgenic regulators for engineering pigmented endosperm.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Relevant in pigmented tissues such as pericarp/leaves; defective in target white rice varieties and inactive for endosperm engineering without complementation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defective OsB2 in ZH11/HG1 is associated with inability to produce anthocyanins; promoter-diverged allele Kala4 of OsB2 controls black pericarp in rice.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequence analysis in this study; prior literature on OsB2/Kala4 cited.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Native bHLH determinant of anthocyanin production in rice tissues, but nonfunctional/insufficient in target white endosperm background.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Rice endosperm engineering and natural pericarp pigmentation context.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct regulator as component of MBW transcriptional complex in anthocyanin pathway, based on prior literature context.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Kala4/promoter-diverged functional OsB2 allele for pigmented grain; not used in this study
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate-gene sequence analysis; contextualized with prior locus studies.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Rice varieties including ZH11 and HG1; natural pigmented rice context from literature.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Interacts with OsC1 and OsWD40 in MBW-like complex; homologous to maize Booster1.
Regulation_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Defective allele in ZH11/HG1; exact site not given here. Kala4 described as promoter-diverged allele in literature context.
Regulation_Genes.Key_Variant_Type: promoter variation / other defect (exact lesion NA)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Indirect in this paper; HPLC anthocyanin analysis in engineered lines.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Kala4, a promoter-diverged allele of OsB2, controls anthocyanin production in black rice pericarp according to cited work.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data analyzed for endosperm expression; accession numbers NA.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Controls black pericarp pigmentation in natural rice germplasm (literature context).
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as part of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin biosynthesis genes when functional

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential endogenous regulator but silent/low in endosperm and not activated in engineered line; therefore unsuitable alone for endosperm anthocyanin biofortification in white rice backgrounds.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Silent or expressed at low levels in rice endosperm; relevant to tissue-specific failure of anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Low/silent OsC1 expression in endosperm contributes to lack of anthocyanin production; remained unactivated in PER despite pathway engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Public transcriptome analysis and semi-quantitative RT-PCR in developing endosperm.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A key native MYB component of rice anthocyanin regulation, but not engaged in the engineered endosperm pathway because it stayed silent.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Developing endosperm at 7 and 14 days after pollination.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct regulator, based on established MBW role.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Functional expressed allele in pigmented tissues would be favorable; in ZH11 endosperm expression is low/silent
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: ZH11 and public rice transcriptome resources.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Forms MBW complex with bHLH factors OsB1/OsB2 and OsWD40; homolog of maize ZmC1.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Anthocyanins measured by HPLC in lines where other regulators were introduced.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Paper states endogenous MYB gene OsC1 was not activated in PER line, unlike many endogenous structural genes.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data; RT-PCR data in study; accessions NA.
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Silent/low in ZH11 endosperm; no activation detected in PER by semi-quantitative RT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as part of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Would activate anthocyanin genes if expressed/functionally partnered; not active in PER endosperm

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: High-value transgene for anthocyanin biofortification of cereal endosperm; required component of engineered purple endosperm rice.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Expressed under endosperm-specific promoter in developing seed endosperm; requirement demonstrated in engineered rice lines.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in rice endosperm by rebuilding MBW complex with ZmPl and endogenous OsWD40; absence causes white grains.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression, semi-quantitative RT-PCR, metabolite profiling by HPLC, and genotype–phenotype evidence from lines lacking ZmLc.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential bHLH activator in the engineered system; lines lacking only ZmLc produced white grains, confirming decisive requirement.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Developing rice endosperm (7 and 14 DAP); mature grain pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_Binding_or_Direct_Target_Evidence: No direct binding assay in this paper; inferred direct transcriptional role from established MBW biology.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Presence of functional endosperm-expressed ZmLc transgene
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible positive reinforcement of OsWD40 expression via MBW complex; exact feedback not directly resolved.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate transgene chosen from maize based on known anthocyanin regulatory function and stronger performance of maize regulators in heterologous systems.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Transgenic rice lines in ZH11 and HG1 backgrounds; lines Z#8 and H#9 lacking ZmLc noted.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Functions with ZmPl and endogenous OsWD40 to rebuild MBW complex.
Regulation_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Transgene absence in white-grain lines Z#8 and H#9
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis gene activation, including endogenous structural/decorating/transporter genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC for C3G/P3G; TEAC antioxidant assay
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Maize Leaf color gene encodes bHLH TF. Previous rice endosperm attempts with ZmR-S and ZmC1 failed; here ZmLc with ZmPl plus structural genes succeeded.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-PCR expression analysis; public transcriptome context for endogenous genes; accessions NA.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: No major change in plant height, tiller number, or seed setting; grain weight reduced about 25% in PER lines overall.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated engineered lines showed ~25% reduced grain weight relative to ZH11.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Activated or enhanced 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes, including structural, decorating, transporter, upstream phenylalanine pathway genes, and OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Absence of ZmLc resulted in white grains; PER-Z#3 total anthocyanin reached ~1 mg/g dry grain in full engineered background
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates multiple endogenous anthocyanin biosynthesis-related genes in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Anthocyanin content and antioxidant activity in grain endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Polished grain endosperm and developing endosperm

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Core transgene for anthocyanin biofortification; used to activate pathway genes in cereal endosperm, with stronger activity than ZmC1 homolog.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Endosperm-specific transgenic expression in developing rice seed.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm together with ZmLc and endogenous OsWD40, leading to accumulation of C3G and P3G.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression, RT-PCR of downstream endogenous genes, HPLC metabolite profiling, phenotypic purple endosperm production.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Chosen because it is a strong MYB activator homologous to ZmC1; essential component of engineered MBW complex driving pathway reconstitution.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP) and mature grains.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_Binding_or_Direct_Target_Evidence: No binding assay in this paper; direct regulatory role inferred from known MYB anthocyanin regulator function.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Presence of functional endosperm-expressed ZmPl transgene
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely contributes to upregulation of OsWD40 via MBW complex; direct feedback details unresolved.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate transgene approach; amplified from plasmid p3301(Lc+Pl) based on literature showing strong activity.
Regulation_Genes.Genetic_Population: Transgenic rice lines in ZH11 and HG1 backgrounds.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Acts with ZmLc and endogenous OsWD40 in rebuilt MBW complex.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC for anthocyanins; TEAC antioxidant assay
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: ZmPl is homolog of ZmC1 but has stronger activity, motivating selection for this engineering study.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: PER lines overall had ~25% reduced grain weight but otherwise similar growth traits to parent.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible contribution as part of multigene engineering package associated with lower grain weight.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Multiple endogenous anthocyanin biosynthesis, decoration, transport, and upstream phenylalanine genes
Regulation_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Full engineered lines accumulated up to ~1 mg/g dry grain total anthocyanin in PER-Z#3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates multiple endogenous anthocyanin-related genes in endosperm

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Endogenous cofactor regulator that can be leveraged with heterologous MYB/bHLH factors to activate anthocyanin biofortification in rice endosperm.
Regulation_Genes.Condition_Dependence_of_Regulation: Upregulated in engineered PER endosperm; contributes in developing seed under transgene-driven MBW reconstruction.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports anthocyanin accumulation by acting with ZmLc and ZmPl in rebuilt MBW complex; endogenous transcript was upregulated in PER.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semi-quantitative RT-PCR in developing endosperm and pathway inference from known MBW regulatory model.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Provides endogenous WD40 partner enabling transgenic ZmLc/ZmPl to rebuild functional MBW complex and activate pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Developing rice endosperm at 7 and 14 DAP.
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct regulator as MBW complex component, though not DNA-binding on its own.
Regulation_Genes.Directness_of_Effect_on_Target_Product: direct via direct regulation of key metabolic gene(s)
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Probably upregulated by MBW complex itself according to authors' inference.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate endogenous regulator examined by RT-PCR after engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Interaction_with_Other_Regulators: Interacts with OsC1/OsB1/OsB2 in native rice and with transgenic ZmLc/ZmPl in PER.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Anthocyanins measured by HPLC in associated engineered lines.
Regulation_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Upregulation in PER was inferred to be caused by MBW complex.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-PCR in study; public transcriptome context NA.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Acts as co-regulator for multiple anthocyanin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Co-activation/scaffold component of MBW transcriptional complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports activation of multiple anthocyanin genes through MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Activated/upregulated in presence of transgenic MBW components

Title: Development of "Purple Endosperm Rice" by Engineering Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in the Endosperm with a High-Efficiency Transgene Stacking System
